rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical synthesis of 3-alkyl phthalides via anodic oxidation of 2-vinylbenzoic acids in the presence of aliphatic carboxylic acids as alkyl radical precursors (Kolbe-type co-electrolysis).
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the aromatic carboxylate (2-vinylbenzoate) to an aroyloxy radical and concurrent Kolbe decarboxylation of the aliphatic carboxylate to an alkyl radical; cathodic reduction of the cyclized benzylic radical to an anion that is protonated to give the non-alkylated lactone byproduct.

key_intermediates.name: alkyl radical from Kolbe decarboxylation of aliphatic acid (11a, e.g. ethyl radical 5a)
ethyl benzoate (6), recombination byproduct of aroyloxy radical 4a + ethyl radical 5a
non-alkylated phthalide (3) from H-atom abstraction or protonation of 12

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the 2-vinylbenzoate to an aroyloxy radical that undergoes 5-exo-trig cyclization onto the pendant alkene, giving a benzylic C-radical; this radical is trapped by an alkyl radical produced by simultaneous Kolbe decarboxylation of the aliphatic co-acid, or is alternatively reduced at the cathode to a carbanion that protonates to the unalkylated lactone.

probe_reagents.name: benzoic acid alone (no co-acid)
probe_reagents.observation: Quantitative recovery of starting acid; no oxidation observed, suggesting aromatic carboxylates are not directly oxidized under standard Kolbe conditions.

probe_reagents.name: heptanoic acid alone in MeOH
probe_reagents.observation: Electrolysis gave dodecane (Kolbe dimer) with no methoxyhexane, indicating alkyl radical (not cation) intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: ethyl cinnamate (7)
probe_reagents.observation: Fully recovered after 30 min electrolysis, ruling out direct anodic oxidation of the styryl olefin to a radical cation captured by carboxylate.

probe_reagents.name: N,N-diallyl 2-vinylbenzamide (1g) with excess AcOH
probe_reagents.observation: Double 5-exo-trig cyclization product 8a was detected by 1H NMR alongside non-cyclized 8b/8c, supporting a radical cyclization mechanism.

probe_reagents.name: secondary/tertiary aliphatic co-acids
probe_reagents.observation: Only the non-alkylated phthalide 3 was obtained, consistent with overoxidation of secondary/tertiary radicals to carbocations under Kolbe conditions and H-atom abstraction by radical 10.

probe_reagents.name: allyl ester substrate (2j)
probe_reagents.observation: Remained untouched due to preferred s-cis conformation preventing intramolecular cyclization.

notes: Two pathways are considered for forming the aroyloxy radical: (I) direct anodic oxidation of the aromatic carboxylate (disfavored, since benzoic acid alone is fully recovered) and (II) a redox/SET mechanism in which the Kolbe-generated alkyl radical oxidizes the aromatic carboxylate (favored, pathway 2 in Scheme 4). An alkyl carbocation route capturing benzoate is excluded by the heptanoic acid control (dodecane, no methoxyhexane). Ester byproduct 6 (ethyl benzoate) arises from radical-radical recombination of 4a and 5a. The non-alkylated phthalide 3 arises either by HAT from solvent/medium by radical 10 or by cathodic reduction of 10 to anion 12 followed by protonation.
